name: Beneath the Fungal Canopy
description: The gallery falls away into a cavern so large its far walls are only a suggestion, roofed over entirely by a canopy of bone-white fungus that has grown together into a single vast, ribbed vault overhead. Pale stalks thick as old trees rise to hold it up, and from the underside of the canopy hang long luminous fronds that give off a sick, greenish half-light — the first true light since the Void Heart, and worse, somehow, than the dark had been. Beneath that foul glow the whole cavern floor is a garden of caps and blooms and creeping mould, and the air hangs so still and so thick with drifting spore that breathing it feels like a slow poisoning. Things move between the stalks with a soft, boneless purpose, and the light is just enough to show you that they have noticed you.
ambient_messages: A luminous frond overhead swells and drips a bead of glowing pith to the cavern floor, where the mould drinks it eagerly.
The sick green light dims and brightens as something large passes between you and the canopy.
A slow gust moves through the cavern, and the whole canopy sighs and sheds a curtain of drifting spore.
